exemplar.title: How has Delta Airlinesâ differentiation strategy on customer experience improved its competitive advantage and impacted its sales revenues and profitability?

detailedExemplarData.title: How has Delta Airlinesâ differentiation strategy on customer experience improved its competitive advantage and impacted its sales revenues and profitability?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Consistently relates the key concept of change to Deltaâs strategic shifts (SkyMiles revision, tech investments, environmental initiatives).
Clear analytical links showing how change underpins competitive advantage.
Organization-specific examples drawn from Deltaâs annual reports and investor releases.
weaknesses: Introduction remains descriptive with limited framing of the concept.
Occasional narrative passages break the conceptual thread.
âChangeâ is stated perfunctorily without analytical weaving in some sections.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a strong understanding of âchangeâ and ties it to Deltaâs strategy throughout, matching the 4-mark band for partially integrated analysis. Descriptive digressions prevent it reaching the top band, so 4/5 accurately reflects performance.

strengths: Provides the required number of documents (five), covering annual reports, case studies and industry articles.
Most sources are directly relevant to Deltaâs customer-experience strategy.
weaknesses: Two opinion-based blog posts lack academic depth.
One citation is missing full bibliographic details.
Limited diversity of peer-reviewed or industry-standard reports.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While quantity meets band 4, the depth and range fall short. The generally relevant but uneven rigour aligns with the 3-mark band, so 3/4 is appropriate.

strengths: Selects three recognized tools (competitor analysis, extended 7Ps, Porterâs Generic Strategies).
Applications are clearly tied to Delta (e.g., perceptual map, Porterâs matrix).
weaknesses: Applications tend toward description with limited critical evaluation.
No discussion of model limitations or justification for tool choice.
Causal claims (e.g., training investments) are asserted without supporting evidence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The toolkit is adequate and applied with relevance, matching the descriptor for band 3. Lacking critical depth and evaluation prevents a higher score, so 3/4 is justified.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses a mix of quantitative (pie chart, revenue tables, complaint stats) and qualitative data.
Thoughtful evaluation of risks and recommendations (SkyMiles program).
Integrates loyalty metrics to link strategy and outcomes.
weaknesses: Some data are cited without interrogation (e.g., no margin analysis).
Methodological limitations of perceptual map not addressed.
Assertions lack detailed support or statistical validation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis shows adequate selection and some sustained evaluation, matching the 3-mark band. Gaps in depth and integration prevent it reaching 4 or 5, so 3/5 reflects the workâs level.

strengths: Explicitly answers the research question, linking differentiation to competitive advantage and performance.
Draws on key evidence (revenue growth, loyalty data).
weaknesses: Inconsistent validation of figures (e.g., unclear units for the 61,600 value).
Conclusion could better consolidate findings and acknowledge limitations consistently.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions are fully connected to the research question (band 3) but uneven in consistency with evidence (band 2). The lower strand dictates a 2/3 score.

strengths: Clear IA structure with introduction, methodology, analysis and conclusion.
Table of contents and headings aid coherence and navigation.
weaknesses: A sentence is left incomplete, affecting clarity.
Minor lapses in transitions between sections.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission maintains an appropriate structure throughout, meeting the top band description. Minor coherence issues do not detract from overall organization, so 2/2 is awarded.

strengths: Includes all required elements: title, research question, word count, contents outline, references, figures and tables.
Consistent formatting of sections in line with IB presentation standards.
weaknesses: Empty tables and missing figure captions reduce clarity.
Some figures lack axis labels and source notes.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All required elements are present, fulfilling the band-2 descriptor. Presentation flaws are noted but do not omit required elements, justifying 2/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows IB structural conventions: introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, bibliography; tables and headings aid coherence.  Logical flow is maintained, so âappropriate structure throughoutâ applies.

Sources include an annual report, academic case study and industry articles that are clearly pertinent, but two items are opinion pieces (LinkedIn, CX blog) which lack depth.  Overall relevance is solid and they contribute meaningfully, yet the breadth and rigour fall short of the âin-depth and range of ideasâ expected for the top band.

The submission includes title, research question, word count, contents outline, references, figures and tablesâevidence that all required elements are present, warranting the top band for presentation.
Five supporting documents are listed, satisfying the required 3-5 range.  The list matches the maximum band for quantity.

Each tool is applied directly to Delta (e.g., positioning on the perceptual map; illustrating differentiation focus in Porterâs matrix).  The applications are clear and relevant, but often descriptive and lacking critical depth (e.g., limited evaluation of limitations).  Hence they meet âadequately appliedâ rather than âeffectiveâ usage.

Quantitative data (market-share pie chart, revenue tables, complaint numbers) and qualitative inputs are chosen and used within the narrative, showing adequacy.  Some figures are cited without deeper interrogation (e.g., no margin analysis), preventing classification as âsufficientâ or âeffectiveâ, so the 3-mark band is most fitting.

The analysis links Deltaâs differentiation to revenue growth and loyalty, and it recognises counter-pressures such as high fuel costs.  Nonetheless, arguments are occasionally asserted rather than rigorously evaluated, and integration of evidence is intermittent.  This corresponds to âsome analysis of the research questionâ (3 marks).

The student explicitly answers how Deltaâs differentiation improved competitive advantage and financial performance, synthesising findings in the final paragraph; therefore, the research question is clearly addressed, meeting the highest band for this strand.

The student selects three recognised toolsâcompetitor analysis, the extended 7Ps and Porterâs Generic Strategiesâcovering both external and internal perspectives.  This is an adequate, though not extensive, toolkit appropriate to the question.

The conclusion draws on earlier evidence (revenue growth, loyalty data) and acknowledges limitations, showing partial consistency.  Yet several claims (e.g., 61,600 revenue figure) are dropped in without validation, so consistency is uneven, aligning with the 2-mark band.

Throughout the IA, Delta Airlines is the sole focus; data, figures and qualitative evidence are taken from Deltaâs annual reports, investor releases and customer-complaint statistics.  The competitor analysis and financial tables are explicitly tied back to Deltaâs performance, showing strong, organisation-specific insight.  Some narrative remains generic (e.g., âairlines are under pressure to reduce carbon footprintâ), so the connection is partially â not fully â integrated, fitting the 4-mark descriptor.

The student consistently relates the idea of CHANGE to Deltaâs shift toward a customerâexperience-focused differentiation strategy.  References to the post-pandemic revision of the SkyMiles programme, investments in new technology, and environmental initiatives show sound conceptual understanding.  These examples are analysed to demonstrate how the change underpins competitive advantage, indicating more than a simple mention but not yet a fully seamless thread throughout every section.  Occasional descriptive passages (e.g., history of Delta) break the integration, so the work aligns best with âstrong understanding with partially integrated analysis.â

annotations.comment: Consider replacing opinion-based blog posts (e.g., the LinkedIn article) with academic or industry journal sources to increase depth of supporting documents.
Discuss limitations of Porterâs model when applied to service industries to demonstrate deeper theoretical awareness.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated and focused, guiding the investigation effectively.
Labeling âChangeâ as the key concept is a start, but define and connect it to Deltaâs strategic shifts in the introduction to frame your analysis.
Including the word count demonstrates attention to IB formatting requirements and supports presentation standards.
The table of contents outlines the IA structure effectively, aiding reader navigation and coherence.
The introduction is largely descriptive and would benefit from integrating analytical commentary on 'change' rather than historical detail.
Citation âSupporting document 4â lacks full bibliographic details; provide author, title, and publication information to strengthen credibility.
Stating âThe key concept is changeâ without contextual analysis feels perfunctory; weave the concept into the rationale for document selection.
Expand the methodology to explicitly justify the choice of each tool (competitor analysis, 7Ps, Porterâs) and explain how they address different aspects of the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The competitor analysis references sustainability but provides no supporting data; include quantitative metrics on emissions or initiatives to substantiate this point.
The table titled 'Competitors in the market' is empty and should either be completed with data or removed to avoid confusion.
Figures lack descriptive captions and data-source notes; add clear titles and axis labels for each to improve presentation and comprehension.
Clarify the data sources and scales used in the perceptual map and discuss methodological limitations to strengthen analytical rigor.
Link assertions about service superiority to specific financial metrics (e.g., margin or revenue growth) to deepen the evaluation of competitive advantage.
The link between training investment and reduced complaints is asserted without evidence; include correlation data or source citations to support this causal claim.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The assessment of risks and future strategic recommendations for the SkyMiles program shows thoughtful evaluation of potential challenges.
Format mathematical expressions using LaTeX for clarity (e.g., $$	ext{fuel cost increase} =  rac{1.94 - p_{2020}}{p_{2020}}	imes100\\%$$).
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Support claims about sustainability investments with quantitative impact data (e.g., CO$_2$ reduction figures) to substantiate environmental benefits.
Using the 20% delay reduction statistic effectively links operational improvements to enhanced customer experience.
Specify the methodology and sample size for gathering the 18% customer satisfaction increase to reinforce the reliability of this data.
Consolidate the conclusion by explicitly summarizing how differentiation on customer experience improved both competitive advantage and financial performance.
Bibliography entries are inconsistently formatted; adopt a coherent citation style and ensure all entries include complete publication details.
Consider adding peer-reviewed or industry-standard reports to diversify and deepen your supporting documents beyond web articles.
Move the title 'Figure 2: Porterâs Generic Strategies matrix' closer to the diagram and apply consistent formatting across all figures.
Calculate and discuss year-on-year percentage growth alongside absolute revenue figures to deepen the financial analysis.
Analyze the decline in loyalty program revenue growth (5.25% â 3.8%) and discuss strategic responses to this downward trend.
Integrating loyalty program metrics into the narrative (5.25% revenue growth) effectively links strategic change to financial outcomes.
The sentence ending âhave helped Deltaâ is incomplete; ensure all sentences are fully constructed to maintain clarity and coherence.
Clarify units for the 61,600 figure (e.g., $ millions) in the conclusion to ensure consistency with the evidence presented.
